title: What You Have in Christ
intro: There is a difference between knowing your identity and knowing your security. Identity tells you who you are. Security tells you that it cannot be taken from you. In Christ, both are established — not by your faithfulness, but by His.
reading:
Gavin's outline moves from who you are in Christ to what you have in Christ — the relational and positional realities that belong to every believer.

You are a friend of God. John 15:15 records Jesus telling His disciples that He no longer calls them servants but friends — because He has made known to them everything He heard from the Father. James 2:23 records that Abraham was called a friend of God. Friendship with God is not a presumption. It is an invitation extended by Christ Himself.

You are part of the body of Christ. 1 Corinthians 12:27 declares that you are the body of Christ and individually members of it. Romans 12:4-5 adds that though we are many, we are one body in Christ. You are not a solo believer. You belong to something larger than yourself.

You are a royal priesthood and a holy nation. 1 Peter 2:9 gathers it all together: you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for His own possession. You were chosen. You have direct access to God. You have been set apart.

You are sealed with the Holy Spirit. Ephesians 1:13-14 describes the Spirit as the guarantee of your inheritance — a seal of ownership that cannot be broken.

And Philippians 1:6 gives the great assurance of what lies ahead: He who began a good work in you will bring it to completion. God does not abandon unfinished work.

Romans 8:38-39 stands as the unshakeable conclusion to all of it: nothing in all creation — not death, not life, not angels, not rulers, not things present, not things to come — will be able to separate you from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
reflection: What is the thing you most fear losing — God's love, your standing before Him, your sense of belonging? How does Romans 8:38-39 speak directly into that fear?
abide: Nothing can separate me from Your love. I let that truth be larger than my fear today. I am sealed. I am secure. I am held — not by my grip on You, but by Your grip on me. And Your grip does not fail.
